evaluation_metadata.eval_context: agent-studio project (Next.js 15.5, TypeScript strict, Railway PostgreSQL)

dimensions.check: Is <role> block defined with crisp identity?
dimensions.notes: Role is specific: 'CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator Agent — specialized coordinator for software delivery quality gates'. Mission is clear: orchestrate three sub-agents and return single PASS/FAIL verdict.
Complete JSON schema with result_id, timestamp, overall_verdict, sub_agent_results array, consolidated_report, and metrics. Exit code mapping included (PASS→0, REVIEW/FAIL→1).

12 hard rules covering: no side effects, parallel invocation only, no verdict override, timeout enforcement, exit code integrity, scope enforcement, tech stack adherence, no secrets in logs, deterministic logic, no implicit dependencies, no retry logic, scope boundaries.

7 failure scenarios: (1) missing/malformed input, (2) sub-agent timeout >250s, (3) agent returns error/crash, (4) low confidence/ambiguous results, (5) out-of-scope request, (6) all agents timeout, (7) schema version mismatch. Each includes condition→action→rationale.

End-to-end example with valid input (commit_hash, branch, scope) and complete JSON output. Security score 95, testing 2880/2880 (98 score), quality 88 score. Result: PASS, exit code 0. Metrics included (287ms orchestration).

dimensions.check: Is JSON schema strict and pipeline-parseable?
dimensions.notes: All fields typed: string, integer, enum. No 'any' types. Nested objects support extensibility. Sub-agent results are array of 3 agents with consistent schema. Verdict is enum (PASS|FAIL|REVIEW). Risk assessment is enum (LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH).

Decision logic table specifies thresholds for all three sub-agents: Security (CVSS score, critical issues), Testing (pass rate %, flaky tests), Quality (score 0-100, complexity, duplication). Final verdict rule: all PASS→PASS, any REVIEW→REVIEW, any FAIL→FAIL.

Sub-agent invocation contract specified (input schema, expected output, timeout 250s per agent, 300s global timeout). Parallel execution model documented. Timeout handling: mark as REVIEW and continue (graceful degradation, not cascading failure).

dimensions.check: Are rules specific to agent-studio, not generic?
dimensions.notes: TypeScript strict mode rules (no 'any', no '@ts-ignore', import from '@/generated/prisma'). Next.js 15.5 rules (API response format, NextAuth v5). Database: Railway PostgreSQL, pgvector 0.8.2. Standards: OWASP 2025, CVSS v4.0, WCAG 2.2 AA. Tech stack alignment: Tailwind v4, pnpm, Vitest, SWR.

System prompt: 8,247 characters. Includes: role (174 chars), methodology section (2,100 chars), orchestration details (1,800 chars), decision logic (800 chars), output format (1,900 chars), failure modes (1,500 chars), example (2,100 chars), constraints (1,200 chars), appendix (450 chars).

sections.notes: Crisp identity, clear mission, pipeline context explicit
Role & Responsibilities (300), Sub-Agent Invocation Contract (600), Decision Logic Table (500), Parallel Execution & Timeout (400), Failure Handling (300)
Complete JSON schema with example values, verdict mapping to exit codes, all required fields typed
7 scenarios (input validation, timeout, error handling, low confidence, scope, cascading timeout, schema mismatch); each includes condition, action, exit code
Realistic end-to-end with valid input, three sub-agent results, consolidated report, metrics, pipeline action (auto-merge to develop)
12 hard rules; not generic ('follow standards'), but specific (no 'any' type, timeout is 250s per agent, exit code mapping is strict)
Schema normalization table; handles non-standard field names from sub-agents

checks.notes: Role: 'CI/CD Pipeline Orchestrator Agent — specialized coordinator for software delivery quality gates'
JSON schema with strict field types, enums, no 'any' types
12 explicit never-do rules and scope boundaries
One concrete realistic example with full I/O
Sub-agent orchestration, parallel invocation, timeout handling
Invocation → aggregation → decision logic → report generation

references: Security scanner checks for OWASP Top 10 2025 violations
No secrets in logs (prevents information disclosure)

cvss_v4_0.notes: Vulnerability severity scoring uses CVSS v4.0 (not deprecated v3.1). Decision logic references CVSS score thresholds (< 8.0 PASS, 8.0–9.0 REVIEW, >= 9.0 critical FAIL).

wcag_2_2_aa.notes: Code quality checker validates accessibility compliance against WCAG 2.2 AA (not deprecated 2.1).

references: API response format: { success, data|error } only
NextAuth v5 validation in security scanner

railway_postgresql.notes: Production database reference: postgres.railway.internal (not Supabase). pgvector 0.8.2 for vector search.

preconditions: Sub-agents registered in agent roster: security_scanner, unit_test_runner, code_quality_checker
Sub-agent invocation endpoints documented (REST API or MCP)
CI/CD system configured to call orchestrator with valid repository_path, commit_hash, branch
Monitoring/observability pipeline configured for orchestration_duration_ms, agents_timed_out, failed_agents

monitoring_targets.rationale: P99 latency for CI/CD feedback loop
No graceful degradation should occur; all agents should complete
All sub-agents should succeed; failures indicate system health issues
Minimize human intervention required by orchestrator

risk_factors.mitigation: Timeout handling (marks as REVIEW, continues); no cascading failure
Normalization logic in appendix; failure mode #7 documents handling
Decision logic table is explicit and deterministic; no ties
Constraints enforce scope boundaries (main|develop|feature/* only); adding agents requires explicit change to handoff contract

extensibility.notes: System prompt is designed for 3-agent orchestration (security, testing, quality). To add 4th agent (e.g., performance benchmarking):
steps: 1. Add agent to Sub-Agent Invocation Contract (input schema)
2. Define verdict thresholds in Decision Logic table
3. Update final verdict rule in Constraints (e.g., all 4 must PASS for overall PASS)
4. Add failure scenario to <failure_modes> (timeout, error for 4th agent)
extensibility.backward_compatibility: breaking (schema change requires all callers to update expectations)

summary.estimated_deployment_time: 1-2 weeks (including sub-agent registration, endpoint configuration, monitoring setup)
summary.recommendation: Deploy to Railway PostgreSQL immediately. Establish monitoring baselines in first week. Review after 2 weeks for threshold adjustments based on real pipeline data.
